narration: You signed up for a free trial — totally harmless, right? But that credit card field wasn't there for your convenience. It was there for theirs.
visual: Laptop SVG showing free trial signup form with 'Credit card required *' field highlighted in red on dark background, credit card SVG floating in from right, countdown timer ticking from 14 days

narration: Number one — the forgetting window. Companies set trials to 7 to 14 days on purpose. That's exactly when you're busiest and most likely to forget you ever signed up.
visual: Calendar grid on light background: week 1 days in neutral grey, week 2 days all highlighted red with 'DANGER ZONE' label, '7-14 DAYS SET ON PURPOSE' badge springs in, red banner at bottom

narration: Number two — the silent charge. 48% of people had zero memory of the charge when it showed up on their statement. That's not a mistake — it's literally the design.
visual: Bank statement rows on dark background showing Netflix, Spotify, Amazon Prime — then 'APP TRIAL CHARGE $8.99' flashes red repeatedly, '48%' stat animates in large below

narration: Number three — the cancel maze. Three clicks to sign up, seven screens to cancel. Every extra screen is engineered friction — not a bug, it's the revenue model.
visual: Side-by-side comparison on light background: green SIGN UP column with 3 checkmarks labeled '3 CLICKS', red CANCEL column with 7 X steps labeled '7 SCREENS', 'INTENTIONAL FRICTION' dark banner

narration: Number four — the compound cost. The average American carries 3.4 of these forgotten subscriptions right now. At $29 a month each, that's $1,183 drained every single year.
visual: Four subscription cards spring in one-by-one on dark background each labeled TRIAL #1-4 at $29/mo, dollar counter animates from $0 up to $1,183, '3.4 FORGOTTEN SUBSCRIPTIONS' fades in

narration: Right now, pull up your bank statement and look for any charge under fifteen dollars. Bet you find one you forgot. Save this — it just saved you money.
visual: Phone SVG showing bank app on light background, two unrecognized charges flash red repeatedly, red CTA card 'FOUND ONE? CANCEL IT NOW', 'FOLLOW FOR MORE TRAPS LIKE THIS' fades in
